BMABMDR 0.1.20
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to BMABMDR: Bayesian Model Averaging for Benchmark Dose Analysis
  • Changelog

Function to perform model selection when including a covariate effect (used internally)

Source: R/fun_cov_selection.R
fun_cov_selection.Rd

This function selects the best-fitting submodel

fun_cov_selection(
  model,
  model_name,
  model.none,
  loglik,
  data_asigma2,
  data_dBMD,
  data_all,
  data_none,
  prior.weightsCov = rep(1, 4),
  pvec,
  ndraws,
  td,
  seed
)

fun_cov_selectionQ(
  model,
  model_name,
  model.none,
  loglik,
  data_bkg,
  data_dBMD,
  data_all,
  data_none,
  prior.weightsCov = rep(1, 4),
  pvec,
  ndraws,
  td,
  seed
)

Arguments

model

stan model with covariate effect

model_name

model name

model.none

stan model without covariate effect

loglik

function to compute log-likelihood

data_asigma2

data for covariate effect on a_sigma2

data_dBMD

data for covariate effect on BMD_d

data_all

data for covariate effect on all

data_none

data for covariate effect on none

prior.weightsCov

vector of prior weights for the 4 submodels (defaults to rep(1,4), equal weight for each submodel)

pvec

vector of probabilities for BMD credible interval

ndraws

number of draws from posterior

td

truncation for prior on d in QE4 model

seed

random seed for reproducibility

Value

A list containing submodel weights, all fitted submodels, best submodel

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
fun_cov_selection(model = stanmodels$mE4COV,
model_name = 'E4_N',
model.none = stanmodels$mE4,
loglik = ifelse(data_NCOV_all$data$data_type == 1, llfE4_NI_Cov, llfE4_ND_Cov),
data_asigma2 = data_NCOV_asigma2,
data_dBMD = data_NCOV_dBMD,
data_all = data_NCOV_all,
data_none = data_N_noCOV,
prior.weightsCov = rep(1, 4), # weights for the 4 sub-models
pvec = pvec,
ndraws = ndraws, td = data_NCOV_all$data$truncd, seed = seed)
} # }

Contents
